description_long: This object is a concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. It features a trapezoidal profile with a wide base and a narrower top, providing stability and resistance to vehicle impact. The front and back faces display a large rectangular panel with diagonal yellow and black hazard stripes, enhancing its visibility. Two robust metal lifting hooks are embedded on the top surface, allowing for easy transport and placement by machinery. The surface appears to be made of rough, unfinished concrete, with no visible cracks or significant wear. The bottom edge has a cut-out section, likely for drainage or to facilitate handling.
description: A concrete jersey barrier with yellow and black hazard stripes and metal lifting hooks on top, designed for urban traffic control. The surface is rough and unfinished, with a cut-out at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the hazard-striped panel, two metal lifting hooks, and the cut-out at the base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the trapezoidal profile and the thickness of the barrier, with a visible lifting hook.
description_view_2: Back view, nearly identical to the front, with hazard stripes, lifting hooks, and the base cut-out.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the trapezoidal shape and a lifting hook.
